sketches.mechanism: The `object` declaration creates a singleton — exactly one instance, forever alone, unable to be duplicated or companioned.
sketches.resonance: A singleton named Grief can never be shared or copied; you carry the only one that exists.

sketches.mechanism: The `__missing__` method on a dict subclass is called when a key is not found — it defines what happens when you reach for something that isn't there.
sketches.resonance: Every absent key returns the act of searching itself, because what we do with absence is just keep looking.

sketches.mechanism: A `finally` block executes no matter what — whether the `try` succeeds, fails, or throws. It is the code that runs regardless of what happened.
sketches.resonance: No matter whether we succeeded or failed, the letting go still executes.
